title: Capability — attributes block
description: Validation contract for the `attributes` dict on an Entity row where entity_type='capability'. Register this against capability_type_schemas.type_name='capability' to have SchemaService.validate_capability enforce it. Required fields are the minimum every capability MUST publish; optional fields are recommended and surfaced as 'completeness' on the catalog dashboard. Closed-vocabulary values (lifecycle.state, data_classification) align with seeds/00-core/01-vocabulary.json — the loader will reject values outside vocabulary regardless of what this schema allows.

display_name.description: Human title shown in catalog UI. Title Case, no trailing punctuation.

summary.description: One or two sentences: what this is and who consumes it. Appears in list views, search results, dependency graphs.

owner.description: Owning team name (e.g. 'Core Services', 'Platform Engineering'). Not a person — use `owned_by` / `product_owned_by` edges to point to individuals. Free-form display string.

state.description: Must also exist in the `lifecycle_state` vocabulary for this tenant.

since.description: Date the capability entered the current state.

successor.description: Slug of the capability that replaces this one. Required when state in ('deprecated','retired'). The `replaced_by` edge is the structured form of this pointer.

description.description: Longer-form prose. Prefer attaching structured documents as `facts` (category='overview' | 'dev_doc' | 'security_model' | …) — use this field for short metadata blurbs only.

current_version.description: Semver. Only for capabilities that are versioned units (libraries, services with a published API contract). Omit for runtime platforms or composite capabilities. Version history lives in `bitemporal_attributes`, not here.

package_name.description: Package coordinate (npm scope, pypi name, maven GA). Omit if not a distributable package.

interface.description: Programmatic surface this capability exposes. Present iff the capability has callers — omit for design-only capabilities, runtime platforms without an API, or composites.

kind.description: What kind of programmatic surface this capability exposes. `library-package` for npm/pypi-style libraries described by entry points + install + peer deps (no calling surface itself); `library-api` for the calling surface of a library; `typescript-sdk` for language-specific SDKs that wrap one or more APIs.

spec_url.description: Pointer to the authoritative spec (OpenAPI, GraphQL SDL, .proto). Preferred over inline endpoints — the registry treats the spec as the source of truth.

endpoints.description: Inline endpoint list. Use only when no spec_url exists. The catalog will sync from spec_url when both are present and inline endpoints become advisory.

data_classification.description: Sensitivity of the data this capability handles. Drives downstream security review requirements and PII scanning policy.

target.description: Number for latencies (ms) and availability (0-1); string allowed for budget descriptions.
